fields.summary: Expose a REST API for monitoring the fair scheduler

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: The fair scheduler should have an HTTP interface that exposes information such as applications per queue, fair shares, demands, current allocations.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: Attached xml output from a first cut at this.  Is it missing anything?  Any opinions on whether outputting the whole thing as one chunk (as the capacity scheduler does) rather than making metrics available for individual queues at different URLs is a bad idea?
